perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall receive three (3) minutes of real time (and not game clock time) of display of a Sponsor Mark on LED carousel Signage on one (1) of the main video boards above the north or south end zone during each quarter of each preseason and regular season Jaguars Home Game. During each three (3) minute segment, Sponsor may include up to thirty seconds (:30) of animated messaging. Sponsor shall be solely responsible for any costs related to the animated messaging.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall receive display of a Sponsor Mark on LED carousel Signage on one (1) of the main video boards above the north or south end zone during each quarter of each preseason and regular season Jaguars Home Game.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the part of the original text that defines the usage limit for the carousel messaging to 'three (3) minutes of real time' and the allowance of ':30 of animated messaging' and making Sponsor responsible for animation costs. This creates an in-text contradiction because without the limit set in place, it's unclear how long the Mark will display and if Sponsor will be responsible for paying the cost of animations, as is not further defined. Since section 5 of the Terms and Conditions gives the Club the right to determine and give approval to the Sponsor benefits, it creates a conflict as the section removed could be perceived as a crucial part of the benefits.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. STADIUM SIGNAGE a. Carousel Messaging: Sponsor shall receive display of a Sponsor Mark on LED carousel Signage on one (1) of the main video boards above the north or south end zone during each quarter of each preseason and regular season Jaguars Home Game. a. Ribbon LED Signage: Sponsor shall receive display of a Sponsor Mark on the LED ribbon boards located on the fascia on the east and west sides of the Stadium for thirty seconds (:30) of real time (and not game clock time) during each quarter of each preseason and regular season Jaguars Home Game. The exact timing of each display shall be determined by Club. b. Concourse Signage: Sponsor shall receive display of a Sponsor Mark or Advertisement on five (5) back-illuminated advertising panels at certain locations on the Stadium concourses to be displayed during each preseason and regular season Jaguars Home Game. The exact size and location of each panel shall be determined by Club.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the time limit ('three (3) minutes of real time') and animated messaging details (:30) for the Carousel Messaging in the Changed Text directly contradicts the specification of similar time limits ('thirty seconds (:30) of real time') for Ribbon LED Signage in the Contradicted Text. The original text provides a clear usage limit for Carousel Messaging, whereas the changed text omits it. This omission creates an ambiguity and potential conflict, as the Contradicted Text establishes time limits for other signage types, implying a standard, which is now absent for Carousel Messaging. This absence creates an inconsistency in how signage display times are managed, leading to a contradiction as to whether there are any limits for the Carousel Messaging, as specified in the Original Text.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall receive space to display one (1) full page advertisement in respect of the Sponsor Business and display of a Sponsor Mark in each digital (or printed, as determined by Club) issue of the Gameday Magazine distributed to Club's season ticket members prior to each Jaguars Home Game. The exact size and placement of the advertisement and timing of each distribution of the Gameday Magazine shall be determined by Club in its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall receive space to display one (1) full page advertisement in respect of the Sponsor Business in each issue of the Gameday Magazine distributed to Club's season ticket members prior to each Jaguars Home Game.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that contains 'display of a Sponsor Mark in each digital (or printed, as determined by Club)' and 'The exact size and placement of the advertisement and timing of each distribution of the Gameday Magazine shall be determined by Club in its sole discretion'. This introduces a contradiction by removing the Sponsor Mark from the game magazine; Section 5 of the Terms and Conditions ensures that the benefits given to the Sponsor have to be approved by the Club. Furthermore, removing how the placement, size and timing is controlled could be argued by the Sponsor, that that power does not exist. Thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. DIGITAL a. Banner Ad: During each Contract Year, Sponsor shall receive display of a Sponsor Mark on one (1) banner advertisement in respect of the Sponsor Business that rotates throughout www.jaguars.com (approximately 300x250 pixels) and that links to Sponsor's official website. The exact placement of the banner shall be determined by Club in its sole discretion. b. Gameday Magazine: During each Contract Year, Sponsor shall receive space to display one (1) full page advertisement in respect of the Sponsor Business in each issue of the Gameday Magazine distributed to Club's season ticket members prior to each Jaguars Home Game. c. Social Media Feature: During each Contract Year, Sponsor shall be the presenting sponsor of a video feature that highlights a top rushing play by a Team player during each preseason and regular season Team Game (the "Feature"). The top rushing play shall be determined by Club in its sole discretion. Such presenting sponsorship shall consist of the following: i. A Sponsor Mark displayed in the Feature, which shall be published by Club to Club's official Facebook, Twitter, Instagram or Snapchat account. The post will tag Sponsor's official corresponding social media account. The content, timing and frequency of such social media posts and the social media platforms shall be determined by Club.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'display of a Sponsor Mark in each digital (or printed, as determined by Club)' and that the 'exact size and placement of the advertisement and timing of each distribution of the Gameday Magazine shall be determined by Club in its sole discretion.' The changed text removes these provisions. Section 3b, under 'Gameday Magazine,' states that 'During each Contract Year, Sponsor shall receive space to display one (1) full page advertisement in respect of the Sponsor Business in each issue of the Gameday Magazine.' By removing the clause about the Sponsor Mark, and control of the ad, the changed text contradicts Section 3, which implies a Sponsor Mark and implicitly provides the Club with control. The changed text now creates a potential contradiction because the sponsor could argue for their mark, and not be held under control by the club.

perturbation.original_text: The exact design of the Stands shall be mutually agreed upon between Sponsor and Club. Sponsor may display Signage displaying a Sponsor Mark in and/or on the Stand. Sponsor shall be responsible for all costs associated with the branding and Advertising in respect of the Stand.
perturbation.changed_text: The exact design of the Stands shall be determined by Club. Sponsor shall be responsible for all costs associated with the branding and Advertising in respect of the Stand.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'Sponsor may display Signage displaying a Sponsor Mark in and/or on the Stand.' and changing 'mutually agreed upon between Sponsor and Club' to 'determined by Club', the Sponsor's rights to display its mark are removed and furthermore, the club has full authority over the design, creating an in-text contradiction due to Section 5 of the Terms and Conditions, where the sponsor has to have approval on the benefits provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. CONCESSIONS a. Branded Concession Stands: During each preseason and regular season Jaguars Home Game and Other Events as requested by Club or the Stadium concessionaire ("Concessionaire"), Sponsor shall have the right to display Sponsor branding on (i) one (1) fixed concession stand in the Stadium located in the Bud Light Party Zone; and (ii) the fixed concession stand identified as Concession Stand 118 on the Stadium concourse (collectively, the "Stands"). The exact size and location of the Stands shall be determined by Club. The exact design of the Stands shall be determined by Club. Sponsor shall be responsible for all costs associated with the branding and Advertising in respect of the Stand. b. Vending: Subject to the Concessions Agreement (as defined herein), Sponsor shall have the right to have its food products sold or otherwise distributed from the Stands and/or certain general concessions areas at the Stadium determined by Club or the Concessionaire. For purposes of clarity, nothing in this Agreement grants Sponsor the right to operate the Stand or otherwise sell or distribute food products from or within the Stand.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Sponsor's right to display its mark ('Sponsor may display Signage displaying a Sponsor Mark in and/or on the Stand.') and gives the Club full authority over the design ('The exact design of the Stands shall be determined by Club.'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, specifically section 5, where the Sponsor has the right to display Sponsor branding on the Stands. While the contradicted text states 'The exact design of the Stands shall be determined by Club', the explanation indicates this is in contradiction with the sponsor's right to display branding. The removal of the sponsor's right in the changed text now contradicts section 5a, as the sponsor no longer has control over their branding on the stand, making the terms incompatible.
